Computer Science.description: Focuses on designing and building computer systems, from smart phones to complex databases. Offers specialized research labs in cybersecurity, game engineering, and big data analysis.

Economics.description: Prepares students to understand decision-making, market structures, and global economic relationships. Emphasizes excellent student-faculty rapport with faculty engaged in active research.

Psychology.description: Approaches psychology from multiple perspectives including cognitive, social, personality, neuroscience, and developmental frameworks. Emphasizes the scientific basis of psychology.

Business.description: STEM-certified degree integrating business education with liberal arts grounding. Emphasizes global perspectives, social responsibility, and professional ethics.

Film and Television.description: The Kanbar Institute of Film and Television has been a major cultural force since 1965.

Applied Psychology.description: Interdisciplinary program teaching students to bridge psychological theory, research, and practice to solve everyday problems in a multicultural world.

Liberal Studies Core.description: A two-year interdisciplinary global curriculum from which students directly transition to 90 majors at NYU to complete their bachelor's degree

Note: Programs are listed under partner.description: 13 combined majors pairing Global Public Health with disciplines across NYU schools
